title: Adenogramma teretifolia
intro: Adenogramma teretifolia, also known as the "Matala plant", is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. The Matala plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Adenogramma teretifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the Matala plant is often prepared as a tea or infused in oil, and it is also used in the production of traditional folk remedies, such as balms and salves.
